old_claim.note: Early Boats is an earlier historical predecessor or foundation, not a one-to-one engineering dependency.

new_claim.note: Earlier watercraft are the historical boat lineage for refined dugouts; this is not a claim that every earlier boat form directly caused the Pesse canoe.

source_shape.source_locator: McGrail's prehistoric watercraft survey treats dugouts/logboats as early boat forms and identifies the Pesse canoe as an early recovered dugout.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source places the Pesse-style dugout in early boat/logboat history.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The edge is kept as historical lineage only: early boat traditions contextualize the refined dugout without turning every earlier watercraft into a required engineering dependency.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Preserved: wooden_dugout_canoe_refinement keeps early_boats as a historical predecessor with a narrowed source-backed note.
would_reject_if: The node is narrowed to the Pesse artifact alone and no longer represents a watercraft lineage.
A stronger source shows dugouts should be modeled independently from earlier boat forms.
The early_boats node is later split into raft, hide-boat, and logboat branches that require a more specific predecessor edge.
short_reason: Early boats are lineage context, not a required mechanism.
